doc.content:
# Grime Time - product requirements (exterior cleaning)
## Business context Grime Time is an **exterior cleaning** business. The site must support marketing, trust, and **operational workflows**: capturing leads, following up, coordinating the team, quoting, billing, and customer account management. The team wants **one primary place to log in** to work, with Payload acting as the core system of record rather than outsourcing the customer lifecycle to a third-party CRM.
## Product vision - **Transparency**: GitHub repo may stay **public**; do not commit secrets or customer PII. Planning is high-level and good-faith; pragmatic ops detail belongs in CRM or private docs when needed. - **Public face**: Professional landing and key pages on **Vercel**, content-managed, fast SEO. - **Operations hub**: Authenticated area (starting with **Payload CMS admin** and/or custom routes) where the team manages customers, sees intake, and follows documented workflows. - **In-house CRM first**: Build the customer lifecycle directly in Payload collections, globals, jobs, and custom routes. Use specialist providers only for narrow rails like payments and email delivery. - **Forms & CRM**: Lead forms on the site feed a defined pipeline; contacts must be trackable with follow-up discipline. - **Comms**: Use **Resend** for delivery; sequence state, follow-up logic, and activity history belong in Payload. - **Scheduling**: Easy **calendar booking** for estimates or service (embed vs native - TBD). - **Billing**: Use **Stripe** for payments, subscriptions, and webhooks, while keeping invoice/business meaning in Payload. - **Calls / quotes intake**: Capture call-backs and quote requests; keep the public homepage estimator fast, but allow it to optionally collect a small series of customer job photos when that helps scope. **Quotes are internal** (feature-flagged, staff-only) until you choose to expose them. **Full quoting engine** (fees, labor/material, margin, Texas tax per CPA, forecasting) is planned in `quote-system-and-texas-compliance.md`. - **Team secrets (sensitive)**: Long-term need to **store and maintain passwords/API keys** for third-party tools in a **controlled, auditable** way. *Implementation options must be security-reviewed* (dedicated vault, encrypted fields, SSO, or external password manager with process). Not committed in this PRD-see open decisions.
## Technical direction (agreed for planning) | Area | Choice | |------|--------| | App shell | **Payload CMS** using the official **[with-vercel-website](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website)** template (Next.js App Router, layout builder, SEO, drafts, etc.). | | Database | **Supabase** as the **PostgreSQL** provider (Payload Postgres adapter + connection string from Supabase). Not Neon for this project. | | Hosting | **Vercel** for the Next.js + Payload deployment. | | Planning | RepoPlanner `.planning/` contract; CLI from repo root. |
**Note:** The Payload Vercel template often documents Neon + Blob; we will **swap the DB** to Supabase Postgres and align env vars (`POSTGRES_URL` / `DATABASE_URL` per Payload docs). Vercel Blob vs Supabase Storage can be decided during build.
## Initial users (bootstrap) Create **three** admin-facing identities for development and early admin (adjust domains for production):
| Email | Role (TBD) | |-------|------------| | `bg@grimetime.app` | Team | | `pb@grimetime.app` | Team | | `de@grimetime.app` | Team |
Use strong passwords in non-prod; rotate before production. Map real people and roles in DECISIONS or internal HR doc.
## Near-term scope (now -> first release) 1. **Planning & documentation** - PRD, roadmap, workflow outlines (`.planning/workflows/`), internal CRM/billing architecture notes. 2. **Landing experience** - Marketing landing + core pages via Payload layout builder blocks (hero, content, CTA, etc.). 3. **Customer management baseline** - Payload collections/globals for leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, activities, and billing context. 4. **Workflow docs** - How a lead becomes a customer; internal CRM touchpoints; who owns each step. 5. **Public instant quote v2** - Upgrade `/#instant-quote` so optional photo attachments can travel with the request, and recalibrate the public pricing model before treating the estimator as a stronger promise.
## Later scope (explicitly deferred) - Full **quoting** system (pricing rules, PDFs, approvals). - Full **password vault** product feature - plan security model first; may start with process + external vault.
## Internal business documentation - **Process docs** (playbooks, SOPs, who-does-what) must stay **off the public site** and out of public search indexes. - **Preferred:** Keep this repository **private**, or use a dedicated **private** repo for ops docs; see `.planning/workflows/internal-docs-policy.md`. - **Alternative:** Admin-only Payload collections for internal guides; or Notion/Confluence **private** workspace-still map "source of truth" in one place. - **Do not commit:** API keys, customer exports, or credentials-use env vars and a password manager (see vault decision).
## CRM and billing model - **Payload is the CRM system of record** for leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, activities, and follow-up state. - **Stripe** handles payment rails; **Resend** handles delivery; lifecycle logic stays in Payload. - **Integration:** Website forms and Payload should write directly into first-party records and background jobs. See `.planning/workflows/payload-native-crm-and-billing.md`.
## Non-functional - **Security**: Principle of least privilege for admin users; secrets never in git; encryption at rest for any stored credentials if built in-app. - **Compliance**: If storing customer PII, document retention and subprocessors (Resend, Stripe, Supabase); **Texas sales tax and service taxability** for quotes/revenue - confirm with a CPA before encoding rates (see `quote-system-and-texas-compliance.md`).
## Open questions See `.planning/DECISIONS.xml` and phase 02 PLAN `questions` for CRM choice, vault approach, and booking mechanism.
## References - Payload template: [with-vercel-website](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website) (layout builder, collections, globals). - Workflow stubs: `.planning/workflows/README.md`
